perturbation.original_text: "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to any referenced Person, any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls, is controlled by or is under common control with such referenced Person. "Business Day" shall mean any day other than a Saturday, a Sunday or a day on which commercial banks in New York or London are generally authorized or required to close.
perturbation.changed_text: "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to any referenced Person, any other Person that is somehow connected, through one or more intermediaries, to another Person. "Business Day" shall mean any day other than a Saturday, a Sunday or a day on which commercial banks in New York or London are generally authorized or required to close.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Affiliate" requires direct or indirect control, whereas the modified definition simply requires the other Person to be somehow connected. This introduces an in-text contradiction as the the new definition creates ambiguity around the term, this contradicts any sections where it must be clearly stated who is an affialiate and who is not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party shall, and shall cause its Affiliates to. at all times, comply in all respects with all applicable Laws in connection with the transactions contemplated by this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each Party (the "Relevant Party") shall not, and shall cause its Affiliates not to, do or cause to be done any of the following in connection with the transactions contemplated by this Agreement: (a) directly or indirectly through any other Person. pay, offer to pay, promise to pay, or authorize the payment of any monies or anything of value to any official or employee of any Government Authority or any political party or candidate for political office for the purpose of illegally or improperly inducing or rewarding any action by such official, employee or candidate favorable to the Relevant Party or any of its Affiliate or (b) take any other action that, if taken by a Person subject to United States law, would violate the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act. Each Party acknowledges and agrees that is familiar with the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Affiliate" with a control requirement. The changed text broadens the definition to simply being "somehow connected". This creates ambiguity and contradicts the Contradicted Text, which requires clear determination of who the affiliates are to prevent illicit payments in connection to the agreement. The lack of clarity creates ambiguity which breaks compatibility between the Changed Text and the Contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Section 5.3 Term and Termination. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date and shah continue in effect until the earlier of (a) the fifth (5t h) anniversary of the Effective Date, and (b) termination by either Party for any reason upon thirty (30) days' written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that no Party shall be able to terminate this Agreement so long as such Party is a Defaulting Party under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 5.3 Term and Termination. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date and shah continue in effect until the earlier of (a) the fifth (5t h) anniversary of the Effective Date, and (b) termination by either Party for any reason upon fifteen (15) days' written notice to the other Party; provided, however, that no Party shall be able to terminate this Agreement so long as such Party is a Defaulting Party under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change reduces the notification period for termination from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days. This change causes an in-text contradiction with Section 6.1, which outlines the protocol for sending notices; this has downstream effect on the effective date of the termination, where if the period to effectively deliver such notice is less than what it takes to deliver such notice.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE V EVENTS OF DEFAULT; TERM AND TERMINATION
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6.2 Effective Date of Notices. Any Notice delivered personally shall be deemed to have been given on the date it is so delivered, or upon attempted delivery if acceptance of delivery is refused, and any notice delivered by facsimile transmission shall be deemed to have been given on the first Business Day it is received (or on the first Business Day after it is received, if received on other than a Business Day). Notice given in any other manner shall be deemed to have been given on the first Business Day it is received (or on the first Business Day after it is received, if received on other than a Business Day).
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the termination notice period from 30 days to 15 days. This directly contradicts Section 6.2, which specifies how notices are deemed delivered. If a notice is sent by mail and takes several business days to arrive, a 15-day notice period might be insufficient, rendering the termination clause unenforceable due to the conflict between the required notice period and the time it takes to deliver said notice as stated in Section 6.2.

perturbation.original_text: Section 2.2 Relationship of Parties. With respect to any Project, regardless of which Party identified such Project, the Parties agree that Lloyds shall act as prime contractor on any Project and that Lightbridge shall have a right of first refusal to participate in such Project as a sub- contractor to Lloyds, and shall be entitled to perform such work which would generate up to twenty-five percent (25%) of the total fees payable to Lloyds as general contractor under the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2.2 Relationship of Parties. With respect to any Project, regardless of which Party identified such Project, the Parties agree that Lightbridge shall act as prime contractor on any Project and that Lloyds shall have a right of first refusal to participate in such Project as a sub- contractor to Lightbridge, and shall be entitled to perform such work which would generate up to twenty-five percent (25%) of the total fees payable to Lightbridge as general contractor under the Project.
perturbation.explanation: The original section specifies that Lloyds acts as the prime contractor and Lightbridge has the right of first refusal as a subcontractor. The modification reverses this arrangement, assigning the role of prime contractor to Lightbridge and granting Lloyds the right of first refusal as a subcontractor. This creates an in-text contradiction with the background section, where it is stated that  Lightbridge and Lloyds can increase their volume of business by cooperatively combining resources. The modification undermines the original intention outlined in the BACKGROUND section.
perturbation.location: ARTICLE II GENERAL SCOPE; RELATIONSHIP OF THE PARTIES
perturbation.contradicted_text: BACKGROUND Lightbridge and Lloyds believe that, by cooperatively combining their considerable experience, expertise and financial and market resources, they can increase the volume of business they can conduct in in the area of nuclear consulting and strategic advisory services in the United Arab Emirates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reverses the roles of Lightbridge and Lloyds as prime contractor, directly contradicting the background section. The BACKGROUND section states that Lightbridge and Lloyds believe they can increase their volume of business by cooperatively combining resources. The modification undermines the original intention outlined in the BACKGROUND section by reversing the roles and creating an inconsistency regarding the cooperative combination of resources. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, as it changes fundamental obligations and introduces ambiguity regarding their cooperation.
